prevalence.source: National surveys on substance use + IHME GBD
prevalence.trend: stable to increasing in some states; high treatment gap

burden.mortalityRate: Elevated (injury, liver disease, TB interactions; indirect)

epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: DSM-5/ICD-11 AUD criteria; assess withdrawal risk and comorbidities

publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Low due to large treatment gap and limited de-addiction capacity

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Often identified late; community and primary care screening is inconsistent
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Detoxification and psychosocial interventions; relapse prevention; pharmacotherapy where available; integration with mental health services
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: High stigma, limited services, and out-of-pocket costs
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Growing de-addiction services and public health initiatives in some states
